title: Segments
subtitle: Race yourself over a route you repeat — every effort, ranked, private. No leaderboard, no account, no cloud.
rationale: Comparing your efforts on a route you repeat is one of the few features amateurs genuinely rely on, but segment leaderboards are a paywalled, cloud, social feature. This is the private inverse: a segment is captured from one of your own activities, matched against your history locally, and shown as your own leaderboard and progress trend — no global board, no kudos, no account.

sources.title: Strava puts its API behind an $11.99/mo paywall (free downloads & device integrations exempt)
Dear Strava, we have a paywall problem that's gone a step too far
